Transcrição Completa: When to Fire Sales Reps

[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "23 When to Fire Sales Reps"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Título com ícones de demissão e gestão de vendedores

[00:02] The question comes up which is when to fire sales reps. It's unfortunate but sometimes it does need to happen. So I'm going to refer back to my times when I worked at Grant Cardone's office and what I saw in his business and I'd really encourage that. It works quite well.

[00:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Grant Cardone's Sales Organization"

NÚMEROS: 25 salespeople initially, 40+ when Jeremy left

VISUAL: Organização de vendas com outbound dialing e lead building

[00:22] So at one point when I came in he had about 25 sales people and a lot of them were doing outbound dialing. They were responsible for building their own lead lists, calling those leads and closing those leads. That's how they were judged.

[00:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Statistical Tracking System"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Parede gigante com gráficos e estatísticas de cada vendedor

[00:38] They were also all tracked statistically, meaning every day you came in, there was a giant set on the wall of graphs and statistics. Each one of those graphs was typically the person's sales revenue, outbound dials, connection rate.

[00:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Performance Review Process"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Processo de revisão quando estatísticas começam a cair

[00:55] And whenever those statistics started to fall, that person would be brought in and they would be asked, "Hey, why are your statistics declining? Where are we lacking in the training that we need to provide to you? What's causing this?"

[01:08] They would talk about it at the end of every week versus if somebody's statistics were good, they would encourage that person to help train other staff. They would ask that person about what they were doing well.

[01:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Friday Weekly Reviews"

NÚMEROS: 4 times per month, every Friday

VISUAL: Reuniões semanais às sextas-feiras para revisão de performance

[01:25] Now, to be clear, when the person's sales statistics were declining on Friday specifically, that's when they would bring everybody in. So, four times a month, every Friday, they would bring that person into the office and they would ask them, "What's going on with your statistics?"

[01:40] Or, "Hey, congratulations on your statistics. Keep doing everything you're doing. What's going well."

[01:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "4-Week Warning System"

NÚMEROS: 2 weeks declining + 2 weeks warning = termination

VISUAL: Timeline de 4 semanas com sistema de avisos

[01:50] If two weeks in a row the person's statistics were going down, they would then let the person know on that second week that if there was two more weeks of consecutive down statistics that they were unfortunately going to have to be let go based on their performance.

[02:05] So they'd give the person essentially four weeks of leeway before they would cut the person off.

[02:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Support During Warning Period"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Oferecimento de treinamento e suporte durante período de aviso

[02:15] Within those first two weeks, as I mentioned, they would talk about what they needed. If the person said, "No, everything's fine. I expect it to turn around." Great. Let's see it.

[02:25] Most of the time though, in fairness, they'd ask, "Hey, this is what I'm currently lacking or this is what I'm currently struggling with. This is where I think I could use some improvement." And that would be provided to them.

[02:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Bottom 10% Monthly Elimination"

NÚMEROS: 10% monthly elimination

VISUAL: Sistema de eliminação dos 10% piores mensalmente

[02:42] Up to four weeks was as long as they'd tolerate. Typically, on a month-to-month basis, the bottom 10% of the sales organization would get let go. And that's just kind of how it continued to trend month over month.

[02:55] They ended up with more salespeople by the time I left there. They had about 40ish by the time that I left. I believe they have more today.

[03:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Exception: All Statistics Trending Up"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Exceção quando todas as estatísticas estão subindo

[03:08] The sales people weren't really against it. And to be fair, I want to point out that if all of the sales team statistics were trending up, they would not cut the bottom 10%.

[03:18] Typically, there's at least 10% of the sales organization that does have statistics that are trending down.

[03:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Business Owner's Loss"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Desperdício de leads pagos = perda de receita

[03:28] And remember, when you as a business owner are spending money on advertising to generate leads, it is not only a loss of the salesperson losing out, it's a loss of you as the business owner losing out on revenue because your salespeople are essentially wasting opportunities.

[03:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Key Principle: Track Statistics"

NÚMEROS: 4 weeks tolerance

VISUAL: Importância do tracking estatístico para decisões justas

[03:45] So, it's important that you get them out fast. About four weeks on average is what Grant would tolerate. I'd encourage the same for everybody else's organizations. But remember, you can't just guess who's doing good and bad. He tracked all of their statistics in order to judge who was good and who was bad. Don't forget that part.

🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy revela o sistema BRUTAL mas justo do Grant Cardone! Enquanto empresas fracas mantêm vendedores medíocres por meses, Grant usa dados IMPLACÁVEIS para eliminar fraqueza rapidamente. O sistema de 4 semanas é GENIAL - oferece suporte mas não tolera mediocridade prolongada. Jeremy entende que manter vendedores ruins = DESPERDIÇAR leads pagos = QUEIMAR dinheiro. Bottom 10% monthly elimination é pura DARWINISMO corporativo!
